title: The Throuple

jokes.text: Oh, I didn't recognize him clothed.
jokes.setup: David's family spots someone he knows
jokes.punchline: Implies David usually sees this person naked
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character-specific humor. Reveals David's intimate relationship casually while creating immediate awkwardness with family present.

jokes.text: David, is he here to s...
jokes.setup: Johnny about to ask inappropriate question
jokes.punchline: Cuts himself off before saying 'sex'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny's discomfort with his son's sexuality.

jokes.text: Oh my God, I literally told him to never come back here while you people were home.
jokes.punchline: Reveals he specifically instructed Jake to avoid his family
jokes.explanation: Good revelation of David's shame about his family meeting his hookups. 'You people' is particularly funny.

jokes.text: I just thought after last week, we decided to avoid the motel. Like, generally speaking.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to avoiding the motel after previous encounter
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from understated reference to awkward previous encounter.

jokes.text: David, what should we do? Should we be walking ahead, how far ahead should we be walking?
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to navigate awkward social situation
jokes.punchline: Overthinking the logistics of giving them privacy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Johnny character moment - trying to be considerate but making it more awkward.

jokes.text: I'm just gonna walk, 'cause I don't even care what's going on right now. Keep walking, okay?! Walk, walk, walk!
jokes.setup: Moira claiming not to care about the situation
jokes.punchline: Escalating into frantic commands to keep walking
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Moira's discomfort despite claiming not to care.

jokes.text: I'm tasting metal, for some reason.
jokes.setup: David processing that Jake isn't there for him
jokes.punchline: Bizarre physical symptom of emotional distress
jokes.explanation: Perfectly weird David response. Specific, unexpected physical manifestation of jealousy.

jokes.text: Well, I don't think Jake is here to see David. We can hear you. Very clearly.
jokes.punchline: David pointing out they can hear everything
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward family moment. Not particularly original but functional.

jokes.text: Don't worry, David, I've been in this situation like, the most times, except I'm always the girl getting into the truck.
jokes.punchline: Reveals her extensive experience being the other woman
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis line - trying to help but revealing her own questionable past. 'The most times' is great phrasing.

jokes.text: The least they could do is offer us a ride!
jokes.setup: Jake and Stevie driving away together
jokes.punchline: Alexis expects them to offer a ride to the people they just ditched
jokes.explanation: Absurd Alexis logic - missing the point entirely while focusing on convenience.

jokes.text: Yes, Twyla, I think I'll have the eggs, and brown toast, over easy, The eggs, not the toast.
jokes.punchline: Clarifying that eggs should be over easy, not the toast
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay humor showing Johnny's precision with language.

jokes.text: Well, I went whitewater rafting, Oh no, well, that'll do it, I mean... Anyway, it's fine now. Yesterday it was this stabbing pain, but today it's more like a really intense throbbing.
jokes.punchline: Oversharing graphic details about her pain
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Twyla's oversharing tendency, though a bit verbose.

jokes.text: Uh, who picked through my cake, and only left the icing? Who eats the icing?! What kind of barnyard were you raised in, where you just eat someone else's food?!
jokes.setup: David discovering his cake has been picked at
jokes.punchline: Escalating from cake complaint to questioning upbringing
jokes.explanation: Good David escalation over trivial matter. 'Barnyard' is a nice character-specific insult.

jokes.text: So you can share a boyfriend, but you can't share a piece of cake?
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the contradiction with boyfriend sharing
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and contradiction. Perfect economy and logic that exposes David's hypocrisy.

jokes.text: I'm not sharing a boyfriend, Stevie and I are both dating Jake, at the same time, like sexually evolved human beings.
jokes.punchline: Pretentious description of what's essentially sharing
jokes.explanation: Perfect David pretentiousness - making something simple sound sophisticated.

jokes.text: We're not a 'throuple!'
jokes.punchline: David's horrified rejection of the label
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's denial, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: When it comes to three people, David, there's always a favorite. It's just like how Mom likes you more.
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of family favoritism
jokes.explanation: Brilliant casual delivery of heavy family dysfunction. The analogy works perfectly.

jokes.text: That's not true, and please don't include Mom in an analogy about my relationship, thank you!
jokes.punchline: David's horrified reaction to mixing family and sex life
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing David's boundaries and discomfort.

jokes.text: David, lunch today? - No. - Oh, okay. Well, I'll dine alone. See?! See what?
jokes.punchline: Moira immediately choosing to eat alone when David declines
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy timing proving Alexis's point immediately.

jokes.text: Alexis, I would be absolutely glee-ridden if you would have lunch with me.
jokes.setup: Moira trying to prove she doesn't favor David
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic language for a simple lunch invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira vocabulary - 'glee-ridden' is wonderfully overwrought.

jokes.text: Don't wanna set a bad example for the rest of the motel staff. I thought you were the only one that worked there. Yeah, that's another good reason to go.
jokes.setup: Stevie making excuse about motel staff
jokes.punchline: Admitting she's the only staff member, making excuse even weaker
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie deadpan delivery. Logic falls apart immediately but she doubles down.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's just very fragile. - He's like a snowflake.
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to a delicate snowflake
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective metaphor for David's personality. Good economy.

jokes.text: Maybe I can get the kitchen to whip up a fresh batch of eggs for us volunteers.
jokes.punchline: Calling himself a volunteer while expecting special treatment
jokes.explanation: Shows Johnny's entitlement even while 'helping' - still expecting perks.

jokes.text: No, I hear your concern, it's just apparently what geese do. No, it's not the town's problem, You're just going to have to watch where you step.
jokes.punchline: Dismissive attitude toward goose-related complaints
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Ronnie's no-nonsense attitude toward ridiculous complaints.

jokes.text: Well, just because my daughter and I don't do that sort of thing doesn't mean it's impossible!
jokes.punchline: Accidentally admitting they don't spend time together
jokes.explanation: Moira accidentally revealing the truth while trying to defend herself. Good character work.

jokes.text: If you're still there when we arrive, might we... perhaps push our tables together? Yeah, perhaps.
jokes.punchline: Ronnie's unenthusiastic response
jokes.explanation: Good awkward interaction showing Ronnie's lack of enthusiasm for Moira's company.

jokes.text: I took one tiny break to watch two episodes of 'Scandal' with Mrs. McCreary, while she waited for her dog!
jokes.punchline: Revealing the 'tiny break' was two full episodes
jokes.explanation: Good Alexis logic - completely undermining her own defense with specifics.

jokes.text: I try to have lunch with my mom at least once a week. And sometimes lunch will turn into dinner, because we get carried away chatting
jokes.setup: Alexis avoiding lunch with her mom
jokes.punchline: Ted's normal healthy relationship highlighting Alexis's dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Subtle contrast between normal family relationships and the Rose family dysfunction.

jokes.text: You'll get your dressing on the salad, like everyone else.
jokes.setup: Alexis requesting dressing on the side
jokes.punchline: Johnny's authoritarian response as fake waiter
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Johnny taking his fake job too seriously.

jokes.text: Okay, I'm starting to feel like I'm trapped in an Avril Lavigne lyric here. And while I not-so-secretly love Avril, and have paid very close attention to her battle with Lyme disease...
jokes.punchline: Specific Avril Lavigne reference with TMI about following her health
jokes.explanation: Perfect David moment - unexpectedly specific pop culture knowledge combined with oversharing.

jokes.text: And while I not-so-secretly love Avril, and have paid very close attention to her battle with Lyme disease, um, I don't know why we're...
jokes.punchline: David's oddly specific knowledge of Avril Lavigne's medical issues
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random and specific to David's character. The Lyme disease detail is wonderfully absurd and unexpected.

jokes.text: I'm trying to warn you... That you are going to be ending things with him. And I appreciate that, but message has been delivered. Loud and clear.
jokes.setup: Stevie trying to warn David about breakup
jokes.punchline: David completely misunderstanding the warning
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - David thinks Stevie is warning him Jake will dump him, missing that she's warning him SHE will.

jokes.text: Yeah, no, I was just um... I was just doing squats, actually. Ugh!
jokes.setup: David on phone trying to sound casual
jokes.punchline: Obvious exertion from actual exercise during call
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy. David trying to multitask vanity exercise with important call.

jokes.text: No, well, a heavy salad might as well be a casserole.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic comparison between heavy salad and casserole
jokes.explanation: Good Moira observation with characteristic dramatic flair and odd logic.

jokes.text: 'What's your favorite color?' - Black. - That's one of the questions!
jokes.punchline: Alexis catching her with written prompts
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Moira's awkward preparation for basic conversation.

jokes.text: That was a wine tasting tour, and I was seven years old! You could've used the spittoon, That's what the other children did.
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was wine tasting with a 7-year-old, with other children present
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy revealing horrible parenting as if it's normal. 'Other children' makes it even worse.

jokes.text: I don't remember throngs.
jokes.punchline: Alexis's flat denial of Moira's importance
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy deflating Moira's grandiosity. Simple but devastating.

jokes.text: What is your favorite season? Awards.
jokes.punchline: Alexis answering 'Awards' instead of weather seasons
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment with exceptional economy. Shows both characters' values in two words.

jokes.text: Or will you be taking turns?
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly asking about logistics
jokes.explanation: Good awkward Johnny moment trying to understand the situation professionally.

jokes.text: Let's all be... with us. All of us. Together.
jokes.setup: Jake proposing a solution to the throuple drama
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly suggesting they all be together romantically
jokes.explanation: Solid cringe moment but not particularly original or clever.

jokes.text: I think we all need to be in this together. Now, you've been with each other, and you've both been with me. Let's all be... with us.
jokes.setup: Jake trying to solve the relationship tension
jokes.punchline: Awkward proposal for actual threesome
jokes.explanation: Major cringe moment. Jake's earnest delivery of terrible idea creates maximum discomfort.

jokes.text: No I wasn't. Uh, you showed up for the date. No I didn't. You're literally here in the restaurant!
jokes.setup: Stevie denying she was there for a date
jokes.punchline: David pointing out the obvious contradiction
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist denial in the face of obvious evidence.

jokes.text: But if it makes you feel any better, nobody hates me more than me. Generally speaking.
jokes.setup: Trying to make David feel better after relationship mess
jokes.punchline: Stevie's casual self-loathing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stevie - dark, self-deprecating, delivered matter-of-factly. Good character consistency and economy.

jokes.text: Joking when you're way too busy, trying to tell you how they want their food cooked. I've experienced a bit of that myself.
jokes.punchline: Twyla agreeing while looking at Johnny, who was that customer
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony as Johnny complains about behavior he exhibited earlier.

jokes.text: Well, that doesn't even begin to cover it!
jokes.setup: Johnny offering his tip money as reparation
jokes.punchline: Twyla's understated agreement that it's not nearly enough
jokes.explanation: Good final button showing Twyla's understated revenge on Johnny.

tmdb_overview: David and Stevie must come to terms with their new relationship, while Alexis and Moira become anxious when they schedule a rare mother/daughter lunch.
insight: Relationship dynamics drive 45 jokes across cringe and character comedy beats.
